name: imgui-rs
description: Dear ImGui bindings, immediate-mode, popular for game tooling

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish v0.0.5 on 2015-11-30; repository created 2015-08-16.

latest-stable-version.comment: Released 2024-05-05. No newer release yet as of 2026-04-24, though the repo is still receiving commits.

maintenance-status.comment: Commits continue through 2026-01; ~15+ commits in last 6 months. Release cadence has slowed (no new crate release since 2024-05), but maintenance is ongoing.

maturity.comment: Decade of releases, pre-1.0 versioning (0.12.0) but API is stable in practice.

corporate-backing.comment: Volunteer maintainers; no formal corporate sponsor. Upstream Dear ImGui has commercial sponsors but they don't fund imgui-rs directly.

implementation.comment: Wraps the C++ Dear ImGui library via imgui-sys (bindgen).

rendering-mode.comment: Dear ImGui is the archetypal immediate-mode GUI: UI is rebuilt each frame from state.

reactivity-model.comment: Per RESEARCH.md guideline: immediate-mode libraries use the retained-tree reactivity label for their direct state-mutation pattern (the user owns state and calls widget functions each frame).

thread-model.comment: Dear ImGui contexts are single-threaded; UI must be driven from one thread (typically the main rendering thread). The Rust API reflects this via non-Send Ui lifetimes.

renderer.comment: imgui-rs is renderer-agnostic. Official: imgui-glow-renderer (OpenGL). Community: imgui-wgpu (wgpu), imgui-d3d12/d3d11 (DirectX), imgui-vulkan, metal via custom backends. No native-toolkit or software renderer — ImGui always needs a GPU surface.

hidpi-support.comment: DisplayFramebufferScale / io.DisplaySize handle HiDPI; the app is responsible for wiring it via the platform backend (winit handles it automatically).

vector-rendering.comment: Dear ImGui renders via raster triangles with bitmap fonts. Shapes are tessellated to triangles; there is no resolution-independent vector primitive API.

custom-shaders.comment: Because the renderer is user-owned, applications can inject custom render passes and shaders around the ImGui draw data.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: Custom textures (images rendered by the user's renderer) can be displayed inside ImGui windows; full 3D viewports are commonly embedded this way (game editors).

windows.comment: Requires MSVC ABI Rust toolchain on Windows.

linux-wayland.comment: Basic Wayland works via winit/GLFW (not XWayland). Multi-viewport (detached windows) is NOT supported on Wayland due to missing absolute-positioning protocols.

ios.comment: No official iOS backend in imgui-rs. Dear ImGui itself has iOS examples, but the Rust bindings do not ship official iOS platform/renderer support.

android.comment: No official Android backend in imgui-rs. The upstream C++ library has an Android example, but the Rust bindings require custom platform glue and are not routinely tested on Android.

web-wasm.comment: imgui crate has a `wasm` feature flag (imgui-sys wasm build); usable via emscripten/wasm32 targets with a compatible renderer. Not the primary deployment target.

embedded-no-std.comment: Depends on std via parking_lot and imgui-sys; no advertised no_std support.

authoring-style.comment: UI is constructed by calling builder functions on a Ui handle each frame (e.g., ui.button(), ui.window(...).build()).

hot-reload.comment: No Rust-level hot reload. Dear ImGui's immediate-mode nature pairs with C++ Edit&Continue in supported IDEs, but Rust users recompile to see changes.

layout-system.comment: Layout is driven by widget call order (auto-flow via ui.same_line(), groups, columns) with explicit cursor positioning available. No flexbox/grid/constraint engine.

styling-approach.comment: Global Style struct (push_style_color/push_style_var) plus per-call inline props. No CSS-like stylesheet.

theme-switching.comment: StyleColor/StyleVar can be swapped at runtime; built-in dark/light/classic themes. Trivially reconfigurable mid-frame.

accessibility.comment: Dear ImGui and imgui-rs have no screen-reader support and no AccessKit integration. Upstream issues tracking a11y remain open. The 2025 Rust GUI survey explicitly notes 'no screen reader accessibility support'.

internationalization.comment: No built-in i18n/RTL/localization. CJK and other scripts require custom font atlases; complex-script shaping (Arabic/Devanagari) is not supported by the default font renderer.

widget-breadth.comment: Extensive built-in widgets: buttons, sliders, inputs, color pickers, combos, tabs, trees, tables (beta), menus, plots (simple line/histogram), drag-drop, tooltips, popups. No rich-text/markdown or native dialogs.

data-grid-table.comment: Tables API (tables-api feature) provides sortable/resizable columns. Clipper enables virtualization for large lists. Exposed in imgui-rs 0.12.0 with feature flag.

rich-text-markdown.comment: Plain text only by default. Limited colored-text helpers. No markdown widget in imgui-rs core (third-party imgui_markdown exists for C++ but not exposed in Rust bindings).

native-menus.comment: ImGui draws its own menu bar (BeginMainMenuBar) inside the GPU surface — not platform-native. No OS menu-bar integration.

file-dialogs.comment: No built-in file dialogs. Users pair with rfd or draw an in-app file picker. imgui_file_browser exists as a third-party C++ addon but is not bundled in imgui-rs.

charts-plotting.comment: Only trivial plot_lines/plot_histogram helpers in core. Rich plotting (ImPlot) is a separate project; implot-rs exists but is third-party and in WIP state.

custom-widgets.comment: Custom widgets are straightforward: compose primitives or use the DrawList API for freeform drawing. Minimal boilerplate, but you work in Rust bindings around a C++ API, which leaks some rough edges.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Immediate-mode frame loop is inherently synchronous. Apps can run a tokio/async-std runtime on a side thread and marshal results to the UI thread, but the framework itself is sync-only.

system-tray.comment: No tray support. Users pair with tray-icon or similar external crates.

notifications.comment: No OS notification API. Users pair with notify-rust or similar.

clipboard.comment: ClipboardBackend trait lets users wire up clipboard read/write; winit/SDL2 backends typically handle it automatically.

drag-and-drop.comment: In-app drag-drop API (BeginDragDropSource/Target) is built in. OS-level drag-drop (files into the window) depends on the chosen platform backend (winit exposes file-drop events).

multi-window.comment: Multi-window (multi-viewport) support lands via the docking feature flag in imgui-rs 0.12.0; the renderer backend must support it. Wayland not supported for detached viewports.

binary-size.comment: Not directly measured. Release builds are typically in the small-to-moderate range (bundles C++ Dear ImGui statically), but concrete 'hello world' numbers weren't benchmarked in this pass.

startup-time.comment: Dear ImGui startup is essentially negligible; most cold-start cost comes from the windowing/renderer backend (winit+GPU device init).

memory-footprint.comment: Dear ImGui itself is very compact (~few MB RSS for a hello-world). Actual footprint depends on the renderer backend (GPU driver, winit).

idle-redraw.comment: Pure immediate-mode: the UI is rebuilt every frame, typically at vsync. Some applications gate redraws manually (wait for input events) but this requires extra work from the app.

dependency-count.comment: imgui crate has 5 direct deps (bitflags, cfg-if, imgui-sys, mint, parking_lot) — transitive count is minimal. Real apps pull in winit + a renderer which adds substantially.

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate API docs (~79% coverage) and ~21 official examples, but no guide/book. The 2025 Rust GUI survey notes examples use deprecated components and getting-started is 'annoying'. Users often cross-reference C++ Dear ImGui docs.

api-ergonomics.comment: Builder-pattern API with RAII tokens for windows/scopes is reasonably Rust-y, but manual backend assembly (winit + renderer + platform) is boilerplate-heavy. Some rough edges from wrapping a C++ context.

interactive-playground.comment: No official online playground. Upstream Dear ImGui has a WASM demo, but imgui-rs does not host one.

ide-tooling.comment: No framework-specific LSP/formatter/inspector. Standard rust-analyzer applies.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Dear ImGui ships a Metrics/Debugger window (ShowMetricsWindow) and a StackTool — exposed in imgui-rs via ui.show_metrics_window() etc. Serves as a runtime inspector.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the imgui crate as of 2026-04-24.

last-release.comment: v0.12.0. The repo continues to receive commits but no new release has been published in ~23 months.

notable-production-users.value: Primarily used as embedded tooling UI in Rust game projects and debug overlays; less commonly the sole UI of a shipping app. Upstream Dear ImGui powers Tracy profiler, ImHex, RemedyBG (C++ — not via imgui-rs).
notable-production-users.comment: No widely-known Rust shipping product uses imgui-rs as its primary UI. Its niche in the Rust ecosystem is engine/editor tooling and debug panels.

community-size.comment: No dedicated Discord/Matrix. A handful of active contributors (sanbox-irl + occasional community PRs). Upstream Dear ImGui community is large but doesn't map 1:1 to the Rust bindings.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Third-party bits exist (implot-rs, imgui-wgpu, imgui-d3d12-renderer, imgui-glfw-rs) but are scattered and unevenly maintained. A parallel effort (dear-imgui-rs) offers a broader extension set but is a different crate tree.
